hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 5.7%, the market is pricing Connecticut as a long-shot lottery ticket, effectively written off before the season even tips. That's reasonable given their recent NCAA drought, but don't sleep on a program with blue-blood talent recruitment and a coach who knows tournament basketball when they're this cheap.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's 5.7% Title Odds Are a Sucker Bet at Low Volume
daily_take.deck: UConn caught a tiny bid overnight, but don't chase it. The Huskies are a perennial tournament lock with elite recruiting, yet the market prices them like a mid-tier mid-major. $2.4M in volume isn't enough to trust this price. Smart money would exploit the mispricing in lower-traffic books before sharps correct it.
